The Federal Government's official portal explains the regulations for access to the German labour market. The Bundesagentur für Arbeit (BA for short, AA or Arbeitsagentur) (' Federal Employment Agency') is a German federal agency under the Federal Ministry for Labour and Social Affairs. Mission, services and organisation of the German Federal Employment Agency All you need to know about admission to the German labour market. The Bundesagentur für Arbeit, Germany's Federal Employment Agency, is in charge of this online portal, which is intended to assist people in locating and applying to a variety of educational and training opportunities around the nation. Your entries in this form will be sent to the Federal Employment Agency via a secure internet connection (SSL encryption). As a rule, we will answer your request by post, unless you agree to a reply by e-mail. If your matters are about data protection-relevant information, we will answer you in each case by post. For this reason, we ask you to provide your address data. For technical reasons Please select a concern from the field "Unemployment and finding work" Topics can be selected with the arrow keys There’s no guarantee that the Agentur für Arbeit advisors will speak English, so if you don’t speak German, you may want to bring a German-speaker with you to your appointment. So, just to recap, here’s your Agentur für Arbeit / Bildungsgutschein appointment checklist: Personal ID (passport, ID card) Advice and support You live outside of Germany. You would like to find a job or undergo vocational training in Germany. The Federal Employment Agency can help you. Make use of our support – trustworthy, personal and free of charge. Contact The Agentur für Arbeit offer various programs to improve your employability, including language courses, professional training, and workshops. Take full advantage of these opportunities to enhance your skills and increase your chances of finding a job. On our English site we provide the key figures on the German labour market, which are updated monthly, as well as a report on the European labour market situation. The German Arbeitsagentur, commonly referred to as the Jobcenter in English, serves as a pivotal institution for job seekers and employees alike in Germany.
